title: How CIOs Can Securely Innovate with New Platform Capabilities
author: Marla Hay

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: In the not-so distant past, CIOs were primarily responsible for maintaining IT infrastructure and ensuring systems ran smoothly. However,digital transformationover the past decade and the recent explosion of AI have dramatically expanded their roles. Today, CIOs, CISOs, and other IT leaders find themselves ascentral players in driving their businesses forward. This expanded role is leading them to ask several critical questions: How do we continuously innovate and stay ahead of the competition?How do we build and maintain customer trust by protecting the data being generated by all the different systems in our tech stack?How do we optimize operations and processes for maximum efficiency?

sections.heading: Innovate quickly, but with governance in mind
sections.text: CIOs are no longer just keeping the lights on. They’re essential to driving innovation across the company and creating business value through technology. While the possibilities of AI are endless,getting started is another story. CIOs must identify the areas where AI can make the most impact and develop a roadmap to implement these technologies quickly, whilekeeping governance in mind. IT teams only build35% of an organization’s AI capabilities, meaning IT leaders will need to empower responsible innovation across all areas of the business.

sections.heading: Build trust by protecting data
sections.text: Speaking of governance, innovation won’t keep pace if data security, compliance, and resilience aren’t prioritized andbuilt in at every step. In fact, prioritizing these aspects can actually speed up innovation by reducing risks and ensuring smooth operations. While securing data has always been vital, the rise of AI has magnified its importance. Data is the lifeblood of AI systems, and having accessible, reliable data is crucial. In collaboration with the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and other leaders, CIOs must implement measures toreduce the risk of data loss or corruptionand have a continuity plan in place to bounce back quickly from any data incidents.

sections.heading: Optimize operations and processes
sections.text: Reducing costs, automating repetitive tasks, minimizing technical debt, and improving overall efficiency are well-established duties for most CIOs. A decade ago, when the average company used only a handful of SaaS applications, keeping tabs on each of these systems was manageable. Today, companies use an average ofalmost 900 SaaS apps.This increase makes it much more challenging to manage and understand what’s happening within each system, particularly within Tier 1 applications. How does AI affect these priorities? On the one hand, AI can significantly enhance team efficiency and productivity (marketing employees estimate that generativeAI saves them an average of 5 hours per week). On the other hand, organizations often invest in new AI tools without a clear definition of what success looks like. Moving forward, leaders will need to strike a balance between rapid adoption and cost-effectiveness, while ensuring that eachAI investment aligns with strategic business objectives.

raw_html:
In the not-so distant past, CIOs were primarily responsible for maintaining IT infrastructure and ensuring systems ran smoothly. However, digital transformation over the past decade and the recent explosion of AI have dramatically expanded their roles. 

Today, CIOs, CISOs, and other IT leaders find themselves as central players in driving their businesses forward. This expanded role is leading them to ask several critical questions: 

  • How do we continuously innovate and stay ahead of the competition?
  • How do we build and maintain customer trust by protecting the data being generated by all the different systems in our tech stack?
  • How do we optimize operations and processes for maximum efficiency?

Data breaches? Not on our cloud.

Because your data deserves VIP treatment. Lock down security, tighten privacy, and keep your Customer 360 and Agentforce running smoothly with Salesforce Trusted Services.

Explore now

Innovate quickly, but with governance in mind

CIOs are no longer just keeping the lights on. They’re essential to driving innovation across the company and creating business value through technology. While the possibilities of AI are endless, getting started is another story. 

CIOs must identify the areas where AI can make the most impact and develop a roadmap to implement these technologies quickly, while keeping governance in mind. IT teams only build 35% of an organization’s AI capabilities, meaning IT leaders will need to empower responsible innovation across all areas of the business.

Build trust by protecting data 

Speaking of governance, innovation won’t keep pace if data security, compliance, and resilience aren’t prioritized and built in at every step. In fact, prioritizing these aspects can actually speed up innovation by reducing risks and ensuring smooth operations.

While securing data has always been vital, the rise of AI has magnified its importance. Data is the lifeblood of AI systems, and having accessible, reliable data is crucial. In collaboration with the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and other leaders, CIOs must implement measures to reduce the risk of data loss or corruption and have a continuity plan in place to bounce back quickly from any data incidents.

Optimize operations and processes

Reducing costs, automating repetitive tasks, minimizing technical debt, and improving overall efficiency are well-established duties for most CIOs. A decade ago, when the average company used only a handful of SaaS applications, keeping tabs on each of these systems was manageable. 

Today, companies use an average of almost 900 SaaS apps. This increase makes it much more challenging to manage and understand what’s happening within each system, particularly within Tier 1 applications. 

How does AI affect these priorities? On the one hand, AI can significantly enhance team efficiency and productivity (marketing employees estimate that generative AI saves them an average of 5 hours per week). On the other hand, organizations often invest in new AI tools without a clear definition of what success looks like. Moving forward, leaders will need to strike a balance between rapid adoption and cost-effectiveness, while ensuring that each AI investment aligns with strategic business objectives.

Be ready and AI-ble for Agentforce

Dive into the world of AI with both eyes open – here are six essential security steps to prepare for Agentforce.

Get the free guide

How Salesforce can support your AI digital transformation journey

If CIOs are asking themselves these questions, you can bet that their CEOs and boards are asking the same ones. So, how can Salesforce help CIOs and IT leaders provide compelling answers?

With one deeply unified platform, Salesforce simplifies each of these CIO challenges. The Agentforce 360 Platform brings together all the tools and capabilities needed to drive innovation, secure data, and optimize operations. And with Agentforce built in, you can ensure that all of your AI initiatives are secure, scalable, and aligned with your organization’s broader digital transformation goals.

Introducing new, trusted products from Own

If you’re thinking that a platform like this needs a strong foundation, you’d be right. That’s why we’re strengthening our platform with the addition of Own Company, a leader in data protection and management trusted by over 7,000 customers. Today, we’re excited to announce that Own’s products are officially part of the Agentforce 360 Platform. Here’s a preview of what’s new and updated:

  • Backup & Recover: Protect against data loss with automated backups and precise restoration capabilities at any scale
  • Archive: Manage data growth efficiently while keeping inactive data accessible and actionable
  • Data Mask & Seed: Seed your development org with relevant data and protect any sensitive data by anonymizing it
  • Shield 2.0: Monitor threats and automatically encrypt data to help apps stay compliant and prevent data loss
  • Security Center 2.0: Centrally manage security controls and permissions across all orgs, including AI-processed data and user access

Learn more about these new capabilities here, including on-demand demos and other resources.

Your Salesforce security and privacy roadmap is calling…

Watch the webinar to learn how to strengthen data security, simplify compliance, and maintain data resilience for Agentforce with our innovation-packed suite of products.
